title: toxicodendron mysurense
intro: Toxicodendron mysurense, also known as the poison ash tree or the poison ash, is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family that is native to Sri Lanka. This plant is known for its toxic sap, which contains urushiol, a chemical that can cause severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, despite its toxic properties, the plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been reported to aid in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
